Sim: trader_v12 relaxes 5200+5300 to â‰¤3 while Phase 2/3 evidence used â‰¤2; 3-day sim total rises to 28,525 (worse) vs v5 11,526 with very uneven day PnL â€” not a refutation of Sonicâ€™s regime story; stricter gate targets cleaner per-event stats on tape, not this MM pathâ€™s raw total.

analysis_description: Sim ablation: increase only VEV_5200/VEV_5300 one-tick MM per-side size 18â†’24 (v5 baseline); extract/wing/hydro clips unchanged. Motivation: r4_p1_mark_product_cross.csv has n=160+ rows for Mark 22 on VEV_5300 at K5 (dense vs n=11 extract-as-traded rows).
used_to_decide: R4 d1â€“3 total PnL 11526 worse and all â€” identical to v5 (and v4 global +10 clip, v8 wing +10, v10 extract +4). Fills/clip for core strikes not binding in this sim; counterparty-conditioned sizing needs tape-in-state or different policy class.

analysis_description: Sim ablation: same one-tick MM stack as v5 but require VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 spreads both â‰¤3 instead of Sonic â‰¤2 (trader_v12). Tests whether looser book-state gating trades off tape t-stat cleanliness for raw sim PnL.
used_to_decide: R4 d1â€“3 total 28,525 worse / 28,460 all; days 1â€“3: 16,275 / 936 / 11,314 (worse). Large hydro PnL swing on d1; d2 nearly flat. Stricter â‰¤2 (v5) is lower total on this sim path but is the regime Phase 2/3 evidence was conditioned on; â‰¤3 is not a drop-in 'better Sonic' without out-of-sample risk control.
